slides.title: Meet Bandar the Monkey
slides.content:
Bandar was a little monkey who lived in the jungle. He was very greedy - he always wanted more and more food!

Greedy means wanting too much of something.

Have you ever been greedy? Maybe you wanted all the sweets!
slides.tip: Greedy = wanting MORE than you need.

slides.title: The Jar of Nuts
slides.html:
One day, Bandar found a jar full of nuts. A jar is a big container, like a vase.

Bandar put his hand inside and grabbed as many nuts as he could. But his hand was too full! He couldn't pull it out of the jar.

'Let go of some nuts!' said the baker who owned the jar. But Bandar didn't want to let go. He wanted ALL the nuts!

slides.title: Key Vocabulary
slides.content:
Greedy - wanting too much
Jar - a big container for food
Hand - the part of your body at the end of your arm
Baker - a person who makes bread and cakes
Bored - not having fun, nothing to do
Hungry - wanting food
Let go - to stop holding something

slides.title: Bored and Hungry
slides.content:
Bandar sat there all day with his hand stuck in the jar.

He was bored because he couldn't play. Bored means you have nothing fun to do.

He was hungry because he couldn't eat! Hungry means you want food.

Finally, Bandar let go of the nuts. He took just a few and was happy!
slides.tip: The lesson: if you are too greedy, you end up with nothing!

slides.title: Parts of the Body
slides.content:
Let's learn the parts of our body!

Face - we use it to show feelings (smile, frown)
Nose - it helps us smell things
Hands - we hold things, write and draw
Arms - we carry things and hug friends
Feet - they help us walk, run and jump
Knees - we bend our legs with them

slides.title: Simple Past: Didn't
slides.content:
To make negative sentences in the past, we use didn't + the base verb.

Bandar didn't steal anymore. (NOT: didn't stole)
He didn't want to let go. (NOT: didn't wanted)

After didn't, the verb goes back to its base form!
slides.tip: Didn't + base form. Always! The verb does NOT change after didn't.

slides.title: Example: Didn't + Base Verb
slides.problem:
Make these sentences negative using didn't.

1. Bandar stole the nuts.
2. The monkey wanted to let go.
3. He came home early.
steps: 1. Bandar DIDN'T STEAL the nuts. (steal, not stole)
2. The monkey DIDN'T WANT to let go. (want, not wanted)
3. He DIDN'T COME home early. (come, not came)
slides.answer: didn't steal, didn't want, didn't come

slides.title: A Day at the Park
slides.html:
Yesterday, I went to the park with my friends. I ran very fast! My friend Tom fell down and hurt his knees.

The teacher came and helped him. She touched his face and said: 'Don't worry!'

Tom didn't want to cry. He was brave! He used his hands to get up and his arms to brush off the dirt.

Then we all played again. We used our feet to kick the ball. What a fun day!

slides.title: Example: Body Parts in Action
slides.problem:
Match each body part to what we do with it:

hands - ?
feet - ?
nose - ?
knees - ?
steps: Hands -> we hold things, write and draw
Feet -> we walk, run and jump
slides.answer: hands = hold/write, feet = walk/run, nose = smell, knees = bend

slides.title: The Moral of the Story
slides.content:
Bandar learned an important lesson:

Being greedy doesn't make you happy. If you try to take everything, you might end up with nothing!

It is better to take what you need and share with others.

Bandar didn't steal anymore after that day. He was much happier!

slides.title: Let's Review!
points: 🐒 Bandar was a greedy monkey who got his hand stuck in a jar
📖 Key words: greedy, jar, hand, baker, bored, hungry, let go
🏃 Body parts: face, nose, hands, arms, feet, knees
📖 Didn't + base verb: didn't steal (NOT didn't stole)
📖 After 'didn't', the verb always goes back to its base form
💡 Moral: being greedy doesn't make you happy!
